summary: Iter48 themed crawl-type picker & label propagation fully verified. Backend CrawlCreate.label round-trips through POST/GET (with and without label). Frontend picker reveals on toggle with 6 themed buttons, defaults to Pubs (active #E01E26), theme selection updates active highlight + switches category (Tacos→food, Brewery→bars). Deal with default Pubs opens PubCrawlDialog titled 'Your Pub Crawl'; Complete crawl badge shows 'You survived the Fork·Fate Pub Crawl'. Shared /c/CODE with backend label 'Brewery Crawl' displays 'Group Brewery Crawl', reshuffle hidden, Share+Complete present. Group ↔ Crawl mutual exclusivity intact; re-enabling crawl defaults back to Pubs. Community counters render.
critical_code_review_comments: Home.jsx is ~1500 lines — consider extracting RevealStage/ShufflingDeck/buildFateCard into separate files for maintainability (pre-existing).

crawl-mode-toggle onClick calls applyCrawlType(CRAWL_TYPES[0]) using stale `crawlMode` var (relies on !crawlMode being true). Works correctly today because of closure timing but is fragile — consider checking the `next` value returned from setCrawlMode.
seed_data_creation: Created 4 test crawls via backend POST (labels: 'Brewery Crawl' x2, 'Taco Crawl', empty). One shared crawl code QKAC4 used for /c/CODE UI test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter48 themed crawl functionality passes. Used 1 Google Deal for ZIP 10001 Pub Crawl flow; remaining daily cap ~intact. Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_crawls_label.py can be reused.
